bead.title: agent: root-normalize fallback cwd-derived worktree path in attempt-process cleanup
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Orphaned helper processes (e.g. a stray `find`) whose cwd is a SUBDIRECTORY of a non-`.execute-bead-wt-*` scratch/execution root under the configured temp root (not the root itself) are never reaped by ExecutionCleanupManager.CleanupAttemptProcesses, so they survive indefinitely (observed: orphan 'find' processes 23+ days old, ddx-a9218e18; design: docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-040-leftover-worktree-process-reaping.md, "Change 1").

ROOT CAUSE
executionCleanupAttemptProcessFromWorkerStatus (cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go:415-437) resolves Worktree two ways. The primary path (workerstatus.InferBead + executionCleanupAttemptWorktreeRoot, execution_cleanup_process.go:387-409) correctly truncates any `.execute-bead-wt-*`-containing path to its root, no matter how deep the cwd is nested. The fallback path (execution_cleanup_process.go:420-426), used when cwd has NO `.execute-bead-wt-*` segment but IS inside tempRoot, instead does `worktree = filepath.Clean(trimmedCwd)` -- the raw, untruncated cwd. Downstream, classifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup (execution_cleanup_process.go:201-332) requires an EXACT match: either ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(proc.Worktree) finds a file literally at `<Worktree>/<metadata-filename>` (execution_cleanup.go:1135), or matchingRunStateForMeta (execution_cleanup.go:877-900) does filepath.Clean string-equality against RunState.WorktreePath (execution_cleanup.go:895). When Worktree is a nested subdirectory instead of the owning root, both lookups miss and the process is permanently classified `preserved_uncertain_attempt_process` (execution_cleanup_process.go:266-272) -- never reaped.

PROPOSED FIX
In the fallback branch of executionCleanupAttemptProcessFromWorkerStatus (execution_cleanup_process.go:420-426), truncate trimmedCwd to the first path component directly under tempRoot (i.e. `filepath.Join(tempRoot, firstComponentAfterTempRoot)`) before assigning Worktree, instead of using the raw cwd. This mirrors executionCleanupAttemptWorktreeRoot's truncate-at-next-separator rule but anchors at tempRoot instead of the `.execute-bead-wt-*` prefix.

NON-SCOPE
- No change to the primary `.execute-bead-wt-*` attribution path (execution_cleanup_process.go:415-419) -- it already truncates correctly.
- No open-fd attribution fallback -- that is a separate child bead (see TD-040 "Change 2").
- No change to orphan_harness_reaper.go's cmdline-allowlist path.
- No change to kill semantics (still process-group SIGKILL via the existing KillGroup).
bead.acceptance:
1. The fallback branch of executionCleanupAttemptProcessFromWorkerStatus (cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go:420-426) truncates a cwd nested under a non-`.execute-bead-wt-*` tempRoot entry to that entry's root before assigning Worktree, instead of using the raw cwd.
2. New test TestExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessFromWorkerStatus_NestedScratchCwdTruncatesToRoot in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go asserts that a process with cwd `<tempRoot>/scratch-2026-orphan/sub/dir` resolves Worktree to `<tempRoot>/scratch-2026-orphan`.
3. New test TestClassifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup_NestedScratchCwdMatchesStaleRunState in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go asserts that classifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup classifies such a process as stale (matching a stale RunState by root WorktreePath) instead of preserved_uncertain_attempt_process, when the matched run state is not live.
4. Existing test TestStaleAttemptProcessScanner_DetectsReparentedDescendantsByCwd in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process_test.go continues to pass unmodified.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecutionCleanupAttemptProcess|TestClassifyExecutionCleanupAttemptProcessGroup|TestStaleAttemptProcessScanner' green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
